1_arena_get_borrow_mode_B1V22-1.mint_then_read_twice: ACCEPTS. `&'h h` is a shared borrow of the affine id, not the OWN-1 move a bare affine place would be; h is unconsumed after the first get, so the second get is legal. Both gets' `ISSUE & (content) at 'a` entries are shr-class and coexist read-only with the &'a receiver; only a &uniq-receiver op (arena_mint) rejects under a live content issue, which is the intended mint-under-borrow block discharging arena_mint's R15 obligation.
1_arena_get_borrow_mode_B1V22-1.freeze_record_liveness: The freeze record is per-binding on h, created at the mint's result-stamp and expiring R7-style at h's scope end; the &'h borrow neither consumes h nor detaches the record, so it stays live through the call and after. A callee-side `&'h ahdl` parameter binding needs no record of its own for soundness: OWN-10 pins 'h inside h's storage scope, so whenever any borrow of the id is live the id binding and its record are live too (either disposition is program-set-identical).
1_arena_get_borrow_mode_B1V22-1.f1_two_arena_through_borrow: REJECT preserved through the new signature. h2 : ahdl<place(a2), u64> passed as &'h h2 where the parameter instantiates to &'h ahdl<place(a1), u64> (ar := a1): TYPE-5 exact-match compares the nominal brand constituent inside the borrowed type — place(a2) != place(a1), distinct OWN-5-resolved binders assumed-distinct — reject at the call (R10(a)-place / TYPE-5). The borrow former is transparent to the constituent comparison; the struct-field variant still rejects on distinct field paths per the ATK-two-struct-rings precedent.
1_arena_get_borrow_mode_B1V22-1.leak_forge_not_reopened: The v2 leak forge stays dead: the result type is still &'a T bound to the arena's own region carried by ar, and 'h appears nowhere in the result, so there is no caller-chosen region to book against; inside leak['o] the content borrow is &'i at the locally-introduced 'i and `return rv` as &'o rejects (R13/OWN-4). A helper taking the arena as a parameter can only return &'a, which is sound (the arena outlives the call and the surfaced content issue continues to protect it in the caller).
1_arena_get_borrow_mode_B1V22-1.arena_move_while_id_borrowed: No gap found. (i) While &'h h is live, h itself is R6-frozen (live shared loan), so the id cannot be moved/dropped to strand the borrow; (ii) the borrow can never outlive the freeze record because 'h lies within h's storage scope; (iii) same-statement interleave `sink(move ar, &'h h)` rejects — 'passed by own' is in the freeze's enumerated forbidden list while h lives; (iv) after h dies legitimately, any surviving content borrow's ISSUE entry on the arena place independently blocks a move under ordinary R4/R6 loan discipline. The brand freeze (keyed to carrier liveness) and content-loan entries (keyed to borrow liveness) overlap with no window.

2_cq5_storage_sentence_B1V22-2.sufficiency: The sentence is verbatim to the demanded fix ('a brand-carrier's internals may reference ONLY storage owned by the carrier set (heap, torn down by the CQ-5 endpoint-drop protocol) — never region-bound storage or borrows') and adds the discharge mechanism: CQ-5 frees the queue's heap storage only at the LAST endpoint drop (drain-on-drop, surfaced [STOR-3]). Together the two halves restate exactly the voided guarantee — no region-referencing internals means R13-escape cannot dangle region storage, and last-endpoint-drop teardown means storage outlives every live endpoint — so 'the queue can never die under a live endpoint' is re-discharged for the declassified class, stated for the CLASS (covering future brand-carrier forms; vacuous-but-consistent for ahdl, whose internals are an index plus an erased type constituent, not a reference).
2_cq5_storage_sentence_B1V22-2.placement: Bound where R15 review must see it: it sits in 5.5 inside the AMD-6 classification cluster — the amendment any R15 reviewer processes to classify a form result as loan token vs brand-carrier — closes with the explicit mark 'a sealed-form proof obligation under R15's hostile review', and 5.7's mandatory re-attack surface (iii) targets the AMD-6 declassification.

3_text_fixes_B1V22-3_4_5.r2_correction: Line 563-566 now reads 'these types are NO LONGER R2-confined — they WERE, under ratified R1/R2, until AMD-6 struck them; the sound reason they are now Sendable is that removal' — the demanded replacement exactly, contradiction with ratified R1 removed. No 'never confined' residue remains in Delta 5; Delta 1's H2/open-question wording is forward-looking, explicitly gated on Delta 5 landing first and on Delta 1's own re-proof ('the Delta-1 review inherits this pin'), so no divergence room within this delta's scope.
3_text_fixes_B1V22-3_4_5.leak_rewalk_in_5_8: Present (lines 693-698), matching the demanded one-liner and my independent walk: arena_get returns &'a T = &'i u64 at the locally-introduced 'i, `return rv` as &'o rejects (R13/OWN-4), no caller-chosen result region exists. The 5.2 line-448 cross-reference now resolves; the appendix record is complete.
3_text_fixes_B1V22-3_4_5.transitive_carrier_scoping_and_result_pr: Both demanded clauses applied: box<ahdl>/Option<ahdl> named as the ONLY admitted transitive-carrier forms, and 'generic positions included' explicitly rescoped to the freeze SCAN ('scopes the scan, not admission'). Result<ahdl, E> is now EXPLICITLY rejected — named in the fail-closed list alongside seq<ahdl, N> and user Pair<ahdl>, all 'reject at instantiation'. Nesting probe Option<box<ahdl>> also dies: admission is the enumerated exception to a fail-closed default, and neither spelled form matches. The 5.8 smuggle walks stay consistent (Option admitted-and-covered by a wrapper-binding record; struct smuggle now a definite instantiation reject rather than v2.1's belt-and-suspenders).

4_regression_suite_undisturbed.fan_ep_forge_spot_walk: REJECT stands. None of the five edits touch [B1V21-1]: cq_new<u64, spsc, 4>() records 'qa : spsc at the mint binder (clause i); fan<'qa> compares recorded spsc against declared mpmc by TAG-1 closed-set equality (clause ii) and rejects citing CQ-3/TAG-1 before the body's clone; TYPE-5 remains honestly insufficient and clause (ii) remains the firing check.
4_regression_suite_undisturbed.sibling_stale_copy_spot_walk: REJECT stands. `let id2 = id1` is still the OWN-1 move of a bare affine place — renames the single freeze record, consumes id1; never two live carriers, so no record expires while a copy survives and set-over stays dead. [B1V22-1] changes only the arena_get parameter mode (a borrow site, not a copy license) and [B1V22-5] strengthens the smuggle dispositions (box/Option covered by the wrapper record; every other wrapper rejects at instantiation).
4_regression_suite_undisturbed.edit_locality: The remaining v2.1 verdicts rest on machinery no edit touched: the four AMD-6 corpus programs (P5, A11, ATK-two-struct-rings, ATK-zero-region-endpoint) turn on the by-declaration token/carrier criterion and ratified loan clauses, both unchanged — and the ATK-zero-region-endpoint residue is precisely what B1V22-2/B1V22-3 resolve; F0.2 rejects at the untouched freeze list; D5 gates unchanged (BRAND-MINT/SEALED-MATCH); the 5.7 one-pass cost claim survives since the through-borrow TYPE-5 compare and the finite box/Option admission test are table lookups — no counting, no fixpoint.

5_fix_application_map.v21_MAJOR_own_mode_composition: B1V22-1 APPLIED FULLY — shared borrow in both rows (5.2 arena_get['a,'h] and 5.6 derivation-2 use_id), rationale stated inline, 5.8 mode-fix note plus the mint-then-read-twice rewalk present.
5_fix_application_map.v21_MAJOR_missing_cq5_sentence: B1V22-2 APPLIED FULLY — sentence verbatim in 5.5, mechanism stated, marked an R15 sealed-form proof obligation.
5_fix_application_map.v21_MINOR_were_never_r2_confined: B1V22-3 APPLIED FULLY — exact demanded wording plus the sound-reason clause.
5_fix_application_map.v21_MINOR_dangling_leak_reference: B1V22-4 APPLIED FULLY — 5.8 rewalk paragraph added; matches the independent walk.
5_fix_application_map.v21_NOTE_box_option_wording: B1V22-5 APPLIED FULLY — only-admitted-forms clause, scan-vs-admission rescope, explicit reject list including Result<ahdl, E>.

verdict: CLOSE. All five [B1V22-n] edits are applied exactly as v2.1's minimal fixes demanded and none introduces a new defect. The borrow-mode arena_get row restores mint-then-read-twice while the place-brand TYPE-5 check fires identically through the borrow (F1 two-arena rewalked: REJECT), the v2 leak forge stays dead (result still bound to the arena's own region; 'h grants no result-region channel), and the R6 freeze plus the &'h loan interlock leaves no window to move the arena while an id or content borrow lives. The CQ-5 storage sentence restates the dropped liveness guarantee for the whole brand-carrier class and is bound into the AMD-6 locus as an explicit R15 sealed-form proof obligation. The R2 correction, the 5.8 leak rewalk, and the box/Option-only transitive-carrier scoping (with Result<ahdl, E> now an explicit instantiation reject) remove the last contradiction and divergence room. The v2.1 regression suite is undisturbed: the fan ep-forge still rejects at clause (ii) recorded-vs-declared bound and the sibling stale-copy still dies on affine move-not-copy; every other verdict rests on machinery the five edits do not touch. No FATAL, no MAJOR, no MINOR remains open. BRAND-1 v2.2 stands ready for the mandated hostile RE-review (5.7) as the next gate; nothing in this close substitutes for that review.
hardest_failed: HARDEST: freeze-gap hunt around the new &'h borrow — every ordering that tries to make the arena movable while its memory is still reachable dies twice over: early-dropping or moving the id under a live &'h borrow rejects (R6 shared-loan freeze on the id's own place); the borrow can never outlive the id's freeze record (OWN-10 pins 'h inside h's storage scope); same-statement interleave sink(move ar, &'h h) rejects because 'passed by own' is in the freeze's enumerated list while h lives; and after the id dies legitimately, a surviving content borrow's ISSUE &(content) entry on the arena place independently blocks the move under ratified R4/R6. The brand freeze (carrier-liveness-keyed) and the content-loan entries (borrow-liveness-keyed) overlap with no window between them.

Wrong-arena launder through the borrow wrapper: bet that TYPE-5 at the call compares only the borrow shell (&'h + head form) and drops the nominal place constituent, so &'h ahdl<place(a2)> slips into the &'h ahdl<place(a1)> slot. DEFEATED: TYPE-5 exact-match recurses into the borrowed type's brand position — place(a2) != place(a1) on distinct OWN-5-resolved binders (assumed-distinct) — and the struct-field variant (two.a vs two.b) rejects on distinct field paths per the ATK-two-struct-rings precedent.

Admission-wording divergence probe against B1V22-5: Result<ahdl, E> is explicitly listed as a reject, so I escalated to nested wrappers (Option<box<ahdl>>, box<box<ahdl>>) hoping 'the two admitted forms' reads loosely, and to the &'h ahdl parameter binding as a third carrier shape outside both the admitted and rejected lists. DEFEATED: admission is the enumerated exception to a fail-closed default (only the two spelled direct forms), so nesting rejects at instantiation; and a borrow position is not a container/generic-instantiation position — either disposition of a record on the borrow binding is program-set-identical because the borrowed id's own record spans every instant the borrow is live, so no conformance divergence is reachable.
